message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: The Tropicale (Palm Springs)
Cuisine/type signal: Californian supper club / cocktail lounge
Why it surfaced in discovery: Long-running mid-century-styled supper club at 330 E. Amado Rd. (Sunrise Park-adjacent downtown). Coral Seas Lounge hosts live jazz Tues/Thurs; widely cited on Palm Springs dining lists.
Discovery sources: https://www.thetropicale.com/ | https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/listing/the-tropicale-restaurant-&-lounge/20315/ | https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g32847-d887095-Reviews-Tropicale-Palm_Springs_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

Based on the search results, here's the information about The Tropicale Palm Springs:

**Opening Year:** The Tropicale opened in 2008, though another source indicates it was introduced to Palm Springs in 2007.

**Owner History:** The Tropicale was conceived by a trio of partners: Ranz Weinert, Tony Di Lembo, and Rob Kurokawa. Founder Chef Tony Di Lembo is the primary visionary behind the restaurant.

**Building History:** The location began as an overlooked auto body shop built in 1975 before being transformed into the restaurant.

# TROPICALE, Palm Springs - Summary

**Review Count & Rating:**
- 1,491 reviews
- 4.3 of 5 stars
- Ranked #22 of 281 restaurants in Palm Springs

**Awards:**
- Travelers' Choice award (consistently earns great reviews, top 10% on Tripadvisor)

**Recent Review Themes (Last 6-12 Months):**

*Positive aspects:* Outdoor patio dining, cocktail quality, specific staff members praised (Kai, Randy, Greg), gluten-free options, festive atmosphere, and signature dishes like the Pupu Platter and fried chicken

*Concerns:* Inconsistent food quality and cooking issues (tough steaks, undercooked/overcooked items), variable service standards, dark dining ambiance making it difficult to inspect meals, high pricing relative to food quality, and staff responsiveness to complaints

*Notable issue:* One review mentions ownership concerns regarding inappropriate staff behavior and alleged review manipulation.

**Sentiment Summary:**
Tropicale generates polarized feedback—enthusiasts praise the "retro and festive" ambiance and particular menu items, while detractors cite deteriorating quality and inconsistency. The restaurant appears to excel in atmosphere and cocktails but struggles with reliable food execution and service consistency.

I found several great resources about the best restaurants in Palm Springs for 2026. Here are some excellent dinner options in downtown Palm Springs:

## Top Downtown Dinner Restaurants

Popular downtown restaurants include The Front Porch, Clandestino, Rooftop 262 Bar & Grill, Crudo, FARM, Tac/Quila, The Thirsty Palms, Eight4Nine, El Patio, and Bar Cecil.

**Notable Downtown Options:**

- Clandestino serves elevated and fresh Mexican food located right next to the Marilyn Monroe statue in downtown Palm Springs, though note that it is closed for summer from June 1 through September 24, 2026.

- Le Vallauris Restaurant is among the most expensive restaurants in the U.S., located in Downtown Palm Springs for a luxury dining experience.

- 4 Saints is perched on a rooftop in downtown Palm Springs and offers a dining experience that feels polished yet relaxed.

- The Purple Room preserves the spirit of Palm Springs supper club culture with live music and classic dining, often pairing dinner with jazz performances.

The downtown area is centered around Palm Canyon Drive, which offers vibrant dining and walkability, making it easy to explore multiple restaurants in one evening.

Based on the search results, here are the best supper clubs, cocktail lounges, and live jazz venues in Palm Springs:

## Top Venues

**The Purple Room Supper Club** is the standout recommendation. It's Palm Springs' Premiere Supper Club and Live Jazz Venue, and this timeless venue has been entertaining guests since 1960. During the 1960s, the lounge was a beloved retreat for Frank Sinatra, Dean Martin, and Sammy Davis Jr., who would gather here for cocktails, impromptu performances, and endless laughs. You'll experience an intimate and inviting room, classic cocktails with a modern twist, world-class cuisine and the best in live entertainment - seven nights a week.

## Other Notable Jazz & Cocktail Venues

- V Wine Lounge & Martini Bar features jazz, Latin, pop, and disco with local artists while sipping wine or martini, and the Monday show from 5 to 8 p.m. is very popular.

- Vicky's of Santa Fe in Indian Wells is a supper club-style venue where live music meets steakhouse elegance, offering smooth jazz, soulful vocals, or acoustic classics, paired with craft cocktails.

- The Cascade Lounge at Agua Caliente Casino Palm Springs offers an intimate setting for live entertainment, showcasing a variety of genres from talented local bands to renowned musicians.

All of these venues offer the classic Palm Springs nightlife experience with live music, quality cocktails, and fine dining atmospheres.

I found comprehensive information about romantic date night restaurants in Palm Springs. Here are some excellent options with romantic atmospheres:

## Top Romantic Restaurants

**Le Vallauris Restaurant**
A refined dining destination in Palm Springs, offering a prix-fixe French-focused menu and a romantic patio setting, housed in a 1927 Mediterranean revival home. The outdoor setting under century-old banyan trees, paired with warm ambient lighting, creates a truly enchanting atmosphere.

**Mister Parker's**
Known by those in the know as the most romantic restaurant in Palm Springs, with a wine list and cocktails not meant for the faint of heart. A fine dining destination with a menu of epicurean, savory favorites with Chef's personal inspirations.

**Copley's**
An award-winning restaurant serving American cuisine infused with hints of tropical and European flavors, located in part of the former Cary Grant estate, offering either outside patio dining with stunning mountain views or a friendly atmosphere indoors.

**FARM Restaurant**
A traditional Provençal-style restaurant in downtown Palm Springs with a menu comprised of traditional European dishes with locally sourced produce, where you can enjoy a meal in an outdoor setting transported magically to the South of France.

**Spencer's Restaurant**
A downtown steakhouse with warm lighting, cozy booths, and a patio, with steaks seared to perfection and paired with an impressive wine list.

These restaurants offer various atmospheres, from cozy indoor settings with fireplaces to enchanting outdoor patios with mountain views, making them ideal for romantic date nights in Palm Springs.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about The Tropicale and Coral Seas Lounge in Palm Springs:

## Overview
The Tropicale offers Casual Elegant Californian cuisine, and features several distinct seating areas: an elegant dining room with plush banquettes and booths, the Coral Seas Lounge (mid‑century bar), and a lush, multi‑area outdoor patio with heaters and intimate corners.

## The Coral Seas Lounge
The Coral Seas Lounge is a hip mid-century style bar lined with sexy black leather bar-stools and reminiscent of the upbeat lounges of old Palm Springs, wrapping around the dining room and leading outside to a lush, tropical, 2000 square foot dining patio where specialty cocktails and old-school favorites are mixed.

## Jazz Entertainment
Every Tuesday and Thursday starting at 5:00, it's renowned jazz musician Derrik Lewis and his trio at the keyboard. Another source notes that live jazz is performed Tuesday and Thursday 5:30-9:30 p.m.

## Cuisine
The cuisine has a light, exotic feel with an emphasis on Pacific Rim specialties, from Chilean Sea Bass baked in Banana Leaves or Kahlua-Barbecued Pork Chops to all sorts of tasty tapas, salads, wood-fired pizzas, and desserts.

content.content:
# Structured Data Analysis: The Tropicale Palm Springs

Based on the provided HTML source, **there is no schema.org/JSON-LD structured data present**. 

Specifically, I found:
- **No `<script type="application/ld+json">` tags** for Restaurant schema markup
- **No address/hours/menu structured data** 
- **No Open Graph meta tags** for social sharing optimization

## What IS Present:

The page includes basic business information in plain HTML:
- Address: 330 East Amado Road, Palm Springs, CA 92262
- Phone: 760-866-1952
- Hours: Mon/Tue 4pm-10pm; Wed/Thu 11am-10pm; Fri/Sat 11am-11pm; Sun 11am-10pm
- Email: info@thetropicale.com

However, this data lacks semantic markup that search engines and applications rely on for enhanced visibility, knowledge panels, and voice assistant compatibility.

## Recommendation:

Implementing Restaurant schema and Open Graph tags would improve SEO, local search performance, and social media sharing capabilities.

Based on the search results, I found information about Tropicale and its award recognition:

The Tropicale in Palm Springs received the "Best of the Best 2025" award from Palm Springs Life. With its party-ready playlist and charismatic staff, The Tropicale has long been a go-to watering hole in Palm Springs.

The venue features pink lights and a palm motif, with lively conversation filling the bar and spilling onto a spacious patio (misted in summer, heated in winter). The bestselling cocktail is a creamy coconut martini, paired with shareable small plates like fried green beans, papaya salad, and Baja shrimp tacos.

input.name: The Tropicale
input.cuisine_or_type: Californian / Pacific Rim supper club with cocktail lounge (Coral Seas Lounge)

reputation.review_volume: Yelp ~2,899 reviews; Tripadvisor 1,491 reviews (4.3/5, ranked #22 of 281 Palm Springs restaurants); OpenTable shows very high booking volume cited as 'Palm Springs most booked restaurant 2023' [UNVERIFIED exact OpenTable review count]
reputation.sentiment: mostly positive but polarized — strong on atmosphere/cocktails, mixed on food execution
reputation.longevity: Opened 2008 (some sources say 2007); ~17-18 years in operation; founding partners Tony Di Lembo, Ranz Weinert, Rob Kurokawa; Di Lembo remains the public-facing chef/owner
credentials: Palm Springs Life Best of the Best 2025 (Best Happy Hour)
Self-cited 'Palm Springs Most Booked Restaurant 2023' on OpenTable
reputation.recency_signal: Recent reviews (last 6-12 months) praise patio, signature cocktails (coconut martini), staff (Kai, Randy, Greg), Pupu Platter and fried chicken. Negatives cite inconsistent food execution, tough/over-or-undercooked proteins, dark dining room making inspection difficult, and pricing vs. food quality. Lounge/atmosphere reputation remains strong.

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Surfaces reliably for 'Tropicale Palm Springs' direct-name queries and on OpenTable/Tripadvisor downtown Palm Springs lists. Does NOT surface in top-result generative answers for 'best Palm Springs supper club / live jazz' (The Purple Room dominates), 'most romantic Palm Springs' (Le Vallauris, Mister Parker's, Copley's, FARM dominate), or 'best downtown Palm Springs dinner' (Clandestino, 4 Saints, Bar Cecil, Eight4Nine, FARM dominate). Notably absent from major editorial roundups (Infatuation, Bon Traveler, Time Out, Cozymeal) in generative summaries.
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: When surfaced, descriptions vary: some accurately describe Pacific Rim/Californian comfort menu with Coral Seas Lounge and Derrik Lewis jazz trio Tues/Thurs; others reduce it to 'cocktail bar with happy hour.' Yelp miscategorizes it as 'New American.' The Coral Seas Lounge live-jazz programming is undercommunicated in agent-readable summaries.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: Weak. The Tropicale's site has NO schema.org/JSON-LD Restaurant markup, no Open Graph tags, and no machine-parseable menu/hours structure. Address, phone, and hours appear as plain HTML only. Site is multilingual (7 languages) and has dedicated menu sub-pages (Happy Hour, Brunch, Lunch, Cocktails, Wine List) but none are marked up for agent consumption. A separate apps.thetropicale.com subdomain exists.

competitors: The Purple Room (direct supper-club / live-jazz competitor, Twin Palms)
Eight4Nine (downtown Palm Springs design-forward dinner + cocktails)
Mister Parker's at Parker Palm Springs (romantic / cocktail destination)
relationships.corridor_role: supporting — established downtown Palm Springs anchor for the cocktail-and-patio dining segment, but not the headline name on editorial 'best of' lists. Sits one block off Palm Canyon, near Sunrise Park, drawing on its own destination pull rather than corridor walk-by.
query_wins: 'Best Happy Hour Palm Springs' (Palm Springs Life 2025 award)

convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Real branded site (thetropicale.com) with multilingual rollout (7 languages), a secondary apps subdomain, e-commerce gift-certificate store, active Instagram (@tropicalepsp), Facebook, paid OpenTable presence with high booking volume, paid Tripadvisor/Yelp engagement, regular Palm Springs Life ad/feature presence, published cookbook (Tony Di Lembo, 2018-ish), and ongoing PR (ICONIC LIFE feature, visitpalmsprings.com chef profile). Clear marketing investment.
convertibility.reasoning: Independently owned by founding chef Tony Di Lembo and partners — no resort/hotel/chain parent. Owner is publicly identifiable, locally reachable, and demonstrably invested in marketing across web, social, OpenTable, and earned media. The visible spend pattern (multilingual site, paid listings, PR, cookbook) signals budget and a decision-maker who already buys visibility products. The gap between strong reputation/longevity and weak agent-readable structured data is exactly the kind of value an AICV node fills, making this a high-probability convertible target.
